title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXXXII. Jingles.
content:
Little Dicky Dilver
        Had a wife of silver,
        He took a stick and broke her back,
        And sold her to the miller;
        The miller would'nt have her,
        So he threw her in the river.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXXXII. Jingles."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
